sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
sources.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

exact_sections.heading: 5. Clinical symptoms and 6. Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

exact_sections.heading: 11.8 Uncontrolled asthma and 12. Referral criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
